q: Fluid statics concentrates on fluids at ___, while fluid dynamics concerns with the fluid in motion.
The study of the flow of liquid is called ___, whereas hydrostatic is the study of liquid at rest.
A liquid is ___ if its density is constant for any pressure provided the temperature is kept constant.
In ___ flow, fluid particles are moving steady in smooth paths in layers.
In turbulent flow, the direction of fluid particles is always changing or ___.
Internal friction in fluids is called ___; and hence, zero viscosity.
In an ideal fluid, the density of fluid is ___.
The volume flow rate $\frac{V}{t}$ is the ___ throughout the pipe.
The equation $A_1 v_1 = A_2 v_2$ is known as the equation of ___ for fluid flow.
The larger the area of the pipe, the ___ is the speed.
Bernoulli's equation relates pressure, flow speed, and ___ for the steady flow of an ideal incompressible fluid.
Bernoulli's equation was first derived in 1738 by the Swiss mathematician ___ Bernoulli.
Bernoulli's equation can be derived using the ___-energy principle.
Bernoulli's theorem states that the sum of pressure energy, ___ energy and potential energy per unit volume... remains constant.
The air flow moving above the top curved surface of a wing has a ___ distance to travel.
Velocity of air flow is ___ along the top surface of the wing.
The ___ effect is a manifestation of Bernoulli's theorem associated with a spinning object.
High velocity air above the roof of a house causing reduced pressure can result in the roof ___ away.
The internal resistance of fluid is called ___.
Viscosity can be thought of as a measure of a fluid's ___ (stickiness).
A fluid with large viscosity resists motion because its strong ___ forces give it a lot of internal friction.
The viscosity of liquids decreases rapidly with an increase in ___.
Viscosity does not change as the amount of matter changes, therefore it is an ___ property.
The SI unit of viscosity is pascal second ($Pa \cdot s$) which is equivalent to ___.
The other way to measure the resistive flow of a fluid under the weight of gravity is known as ___ viscosity.
___ Law states that the drag force $F$ acting upward in resistance to the fall of a spherical object is $F = 6 \pi \eta r v$.
Shear stress is defined as the ratio of ___ stress to the velocity gradient.
Surface tension is the tendency of the surface of a liquid to behave like a stretched ___.
___ refers to forces between molecules of the same substance.
The SI unit of surface tension is ___.
___ action is defined as the spontaneous flow of a liquid into a narrow tube or porous material.
___ refers to forces between molecules of different substances.
Liquids that rise in small bore tubes inserted into the liquid are said to ___ the tube.
Capillarity does not occur for ___-wetting liquids.
The actual amount of rise in a capillary tube depends on the ___ tension $\gamma$.
